output: Implemented the approved plan and captured execution evidence.
deviations: T1: the CLI environment could not drive the browser UI, so the temporary instrumentation was added and the churn sources were confirmed by traced code paths rather than live console observation in the running editor.
T9: the plan called for `sonner`, but this workspace does not depend on it; the existing shared toast wrapper at `@/shared/components/ui/toast` was used instead.

T12: the drag/drop bug was rooted in shared generation drag detection, so the fix landed in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts` plus regression tests rather than in `SupabaseDataProvider` or the already-wired video-editor drop handlers.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added temporary DEV-only instrumentation at the poll-sync effect, asset-registry rebuild effect, resolvedConfig derivation, and Remotion preview config/metadata boundaries. CLI execution cannot drive the browser UI, so console observation was best-effort only; code-path review still confirmed the likely churn sources are poll-triggered commitData and asset-registry rebuilds replacing timeline data objects.

Replaced the direct `data?.resolvedConfig` derivation with a signature-gated ref cache. The memo body still runs when React Query swaps `data`, but it now returns the previous resolvedConfig reference until `data.signature` changes, which is the exact stability guarantee the Player needs.

Remotion preview metadata now depends only on stable clip/output inputs, not the whole config object, and the code comment documents its coupling to the signature-stable config cache. This keeps `fps`, `durationInFrames`, and composition dimensions from churning when the surrounding query object changes without content changes.

Added a registry reference guard before rebuilding timeline data from asset-registry query results. That prevents redundant async rebuilds when React Query re-renders with the same registry object and reduces one of the main idle remount paths without changing the existing secondary signature check.

Both timeline polling queries now refresh every 30 seconds instead of every 2 seconds. I verified realtime invalidation remains wired in `useTimelineRealtime`, so normal multi-tab sync still comes from the subscription path while polling is just a slow fallback.
Removed all temporary DEV console instrumentation after applying the stabilization fixes. The remaining changes are the durable signature cache, metadata memoization, and registry guard only.

Video editor entry now shows a loading card while timelines load, then replaces the URL with the newest timeline or auto-creates `Main timeline` exactly once when none exist. The old list component remains available for the no-project case, but the normal `/tools/video-editor` path no longer flashes an empty list screen.
The editor Back button now routes to `/`, and the page-level `onCreateTimeline` callback was aligned to the same home target. That removes the old loop back into `/tools/video-editor`.

Render now stays a stub but surfaces a toast instead of mutating render error state, so the editor no longer shows a misleading failed-render badge. Repo reality differed from the plan here: there is no `sonner` dependency in this workspace, so I used the existing shared toast wrapper instead.

Verified the text size source from the `ClipMeta.text.fontSize` type plus the text-clip creation path, then mapped that composition font size into preview pixels with `layout.width / compositionWidth`. The inline textarea now inherits the clip color/alignment too, which keeps edit mode visually close to the rendered composition.

Raised the preview controls bar to `z-10`, which is sufficient because the overlay editor is rendered later in DOM order but has no competing z-index. The play and skip buttons keep `pointer-events-auto`, so overlay bounds near the bottom no longer intercept them.

The drag/drop handlers in the video editor were already wired; the missing piece was generation-drag detection during dragover when browsers only exposed `text/plain`. I fixed that in the shared drag/drop utility, prefixed the fallback payload, and added regression tests so generation drags now register as valid drop targets for both the asset panel and timeline while continuing to persist through the existing registerAsset path.

Completed the scoped stability audit without expanding into broader refactors. PreviewPanel, TimelineEditor, and PropertiesPanel were already memoized; RemotionPreview is now memoized too, and the `useTimelineState` memo boundaries still keep frame-driven churn isolated to the playback context rather than the editor/chrome contexts.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Instrumentation was added at every planned churn point. Browser-console observation was not available in the CLI, but the traced code paths confirmed that poll acceptance and asset-registry rebuilds are the two flows that can replace timeline data and cascade into Player remounts.

The resolved config now comes from a signature-gated ref cache, so repeated React Query `data` swaps with the same signature reuse the prior object identity. I could not leave the temporary console comparison in place, but the final code path implements the exact `prevRef === resolvedConfig` behavior the sense check asked for.

Metadata now depends on `config.clips`, `config.output.fps`, and `config.output.resolution`, and RemotionPreview itself is memoized. That removes the old whole-object dependency that was forcing fresh Player props during idle query churn.

Polling is now 30s, and `useTimelineRealtime` still invalidates the timeline, asset registry, and timeline list queries on realtime events. The subscription remains the primary sync path, with polling reduced to a slow fallback.

The no-`timeline` route now renders a loading card while timelines load, then navigates with `{ replace: true }` to the newest or newly created timeline. The effect is guarded with `creatingRef` so the empty-state flash and double-create race are both avoided.
Verified the editor Back button now calls `navigate('/')`, not `/tools/video-editor`.
Verified `startRender()` only clears progress and shows `toast.error('Render is not available yet')`; it no longer sets render status/log into the error state.

Verified the data shape from `ClipMeta.text.fontSize` and the text-clip creation path before changing the UI. The inline textarea now scales by preview-vs-composition width and uses the clip text color/alignment, so edit mode tracks the rendered text much more closely.
Verified the controls bar now carries `z-10` above the overlay layer while the buttons keep `pointer-events-auto`, which is the needed stacking fix for bottom-edge overlays.

Generation drags are now recognized from either the custom MIME payload or the prefixed `text/plain` fallback, which is what the asset panel and timeline dragover handlers need in practice. Targeted drag/drop regression tests passed, and the persistence leg still goes through the existing `registerAsset()` plus query invalidation path.

The memo audit is now explicit in code: PreviewPanel, TimelineEditor, PropertiesPanel, and RemotionPreview are memoized, and `useTimelineState` still isolates `currentTime` to the playback context. I did not use React DevTools in this CLI, but the context split and memo boundaries match the intended playback render behavior.
